answer:
The correct answer is J. Nonresponse bias can occur when certain types of households are harder to reach, and in this context, homes without children or with fewer children may be easier to contact, leading to an overestimate of the average number of children. Since the researcher replaced nonresponding homes with new ones until reaching 50, the sample likely overrepresents homes that are easier to contact, which tend to have more children. Thus, the estimate of 1.73 is probably too high.

\boxed{J}
